area.summary: Defines an area object within the current Therion context.
arguments: <type>: area classification used by the current document
acceptedValues: Line and fill definitions depend on the active project grammar.
options: Use with the current scrap or map block when defining geometry.

declare.summary: Declares a named Therion symbol or value for reuse.
options: Often used for reusable configuration values.

acceptedValues: Use after the matching block content is complete.
options: Typically follows survey, map, scrap, or similar blocks.

equate.summary: Assigns or aliases a value in the current Therion source.
acceptedValues: Any value valid for the current project context.
options: Useful for simplifying repeated source definitions.

include.summary: Includes another Therion file into the current document.
arguments: <path>: file path to a Therion source fragment
acceptedValues: Relative or absolute paths accepted by the project.
options: Use for shared declarations and split project sources.

line.summary: Defines a line object or enters line-editing context.
acceptedValues: Common line types depend on the Therion project.
options: Often paired with geometry editing or line decorations.

map.summary: Declares a map block within the current Therion document.
acceptedValues: Map names used by the current project.
options: Commonly followed by scrap, line, point, and area content.

point.summary: Defines a point object in the current Therion context.
arguments: <name>: optional point identifier or station name
options: Use the station subtype for survey stations.

scrap.summary: Begins a scrap block that groups map objects.
acceptedValues: Scrap names used by the current project.
options: Scraps organize editable map geometry and references.

station.summary: Marks a station point and exposes the station name field.
acceptedValues: Station names must be non-empty when edited.
options: Station points are treated specially in the inspector.

survey.summary: Begins a survey block for a cave-survey branch.
acceptedValues: Survey names and nested survey branches.
options: Usually paired with nested map and scrap content.

th2.summary: Marks TH2-related content or references in Therion source.

thconfig.summary: Identifies the Therion configuration entry point.
arguments: No arguments are required on the keyword itself.
options: Used as the main Therion config file in a project.
